title: Retry sink:throttled requests with exponential backoff
description:
phila-gov-en full crawl produced 22 sink:throttled (403 from API Gateway throttle) failures even at concurrency 2. These are transient — the request was rejected because we burst over an upstream rate limit. Currently we count and continue, but those 22 docs are not in the index until the next crawl re-encounters them.

Add a retry policy: on 403 with body {message: 'Forbidden'}, sleep with jittered backoff (e.g., 1s, 3s, 7s) up to 3 retries before giving up. Could live in postDocument (sink/http.ts) or in the request handler in crawl.ts.

Tradeoff: increases worst-case wall-clock time but avoids needing to re-crawl for completeness.

Spec reference: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-06-phila-gov-en-ingestion-design.md

title: Hand-author search stubs for Vue-rendered top-level pages
description:
/tools/ and /2026-events/ are Vue-app shells: empty static HTML, content hydrated client-side. The Cheerio-based crawler can't see their content, so they're excluded from the phila-gov-en allow-list (TOP_LEVEL_PAGES in apps/crawler/src/parse/index.ts).

If we want them searchable without standing up a Playwright/JS-rendering crawler, an option is to hand-author small 'document search stubs' — short markdown bodies describing each tool/event landing page (titles, descriptions, link targets). Surface area is small enough to be tractable. Could live as a tsx script that POSTs them via the ingest API, similar to ingest-311-kb.ts.

Defer until search adoption shows real demand for finding these landing pages.

Spec reference: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-06-phila-gov-en-ingestion-design.md
Plan reference: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-06-phila-gov-en-ingestion.md

title: checkAndRefresh is dead in production (only tests call it)
description:
services/refresh.ts checkAndRefresh(pool, indexId, threshold) re-queries docs_changed_since_refresh and conditionally refreshes. The only callers are test/refresh.test.ts:59,66 — it is never wired into a route handler or the ingest pipeline.

The config carries refresh_threshold (config.ts DEFAULT_CONFIG.refresh_threshold = 100), which suggests the intent was auto-refresh after ingest crosses the threshold. Either:
  (a) wire checkAndRefresh into the ingest path (ingestDocument already bumps docs_changed_since_refresh; after commit it could call checkAndRefresh(pool, indexId, config.refresh_threshold) — note config is now available in the service), or
  (b) remove it as YAGNI if auto-refresh isn't wanted.

Decide intent before acting. Flagged during the ingest/search config-fetch dedup review.

title: Golden-query eval set with captured ground-truth rankings
description:
Prerequisite instrument for three pending decisions: (a) BM25F-in-JS vs ts_rank_cd-in-SQL lexical pass, (b) exact KNN vs HNSW vector pass, (c) any future MRL/halfvec quantization. Docs (search-performance.md 'Measure before switching') already require this before an ANN index lands, because exact KNN is its own ground truth and becomes expensive to obtain afterwards.

Scope (deliberately basic — enough to keep decisions from being vibes):
- ~30-50 representative queries against phila-gov: mix of exact-token queries (form numbers, street names, 'L&I'-style jargon — the lexical pass's real job), semantic/vocabulary-mismatch queries, and multi-term queries with one rare term (the IDF stress case, e.g. 'parking permit affidavit').
- Capture ranked external_id lists per query in all three modes (bm25/semantic/hybrid) while the vector pass is still exact and the lexical pass is still BM25F. Snapshot to a committed JSON fixture.
- Comparison script: overlap@k and rank correlation between two captured runs.

Context: current BM25 candidate query (services/search.ts ~line 141) is LIMIT 200 with no ORDER BY — arbitrary subset for common terms — so captured BM25 rankings carry that noise; note it in the fixture. Vector pass is exact brute-force scan, so semantic rankings ARE ground truth.
acceptance_criteria: Committed query set + captured rankings fixture + comparison script producing overlap/rank-correlation numbers between any two runs.

comments.author: darren.mcdowell
comments.text: Delivered on branch eval-harness: apps/api/scripts/eval/{queries.json,capture.ts,compare.ts,metrics.ts} + test/eval-metrics.test.ts (12 tests green) + captures/baseline-bm25f-exact-knn.json (43 queries, depth 50, all 3 modes, phila-gov-en, BM25F + exact KNN both current). Usage: tsx capture.ts <label>; tsx compare.ts <a.json> <b.json>. Notable from capture run: long NL queries return bm25:0 (plainto_tsquery AND semantics) so vector carries them entirely; 'PHVIN vacant property' and 'act 77 senior tax freeze' got zero lexical hits — eyeball during pgsearch-qpp.

title: Type embedding column vector(1024); drop dimensionless flexibility
description:
Decision agreed with Darren 2026-07-09: the dimensionless VECTOR column defends against heterogeneous dimensions coexisting in one table — a fork the embedding market has converged away from. Every realistic model choice on Bedrock lands on 1024: Titan V2 (default 1024), Cohere Embed v3 English AND v3 Multilingual (exactly 1024), Cohere Embed v4 (Matryoshka 256/512/1024/1536 — truncatable to 1024). Localization plan is per-language indexes, which is a MODEL divergence (e.g. Cohere multilingual for a Spanish index) not a DIMENSION divergence. A future full-fidelity 1536 move means re-embedding the corpus anyway — ALTER COLUMN in that migration.

Changes:
- ALTER search_segments.embedding to vector(1024). Verify all existing rows are 1024-d first (they should be — single Titan adapter).
- Embeddings adapter: assert/configure dimensions=1024; note that any future model must be configured to emit 1024 (Titan V2 config, Cohere v4 MRL truncation).
- Simplifies the future HNSW path documented in search-performance.md: plain partial index per tenant, NO cast-expression index, no expression-matching fragility in vectorCandidates. Update that doc.
- ef_search>=candidate-limit invariant and pg_cron build guidance in search-performance.md remain valid whenever HNSW lands (deferred: exact scan stays until eval/scale forces it; halfvec is the first lever if memory binds).
- Mind 30s Lambda migration timeout for the ALTER (table rewrite? vector->vector(1024) should be a check-only change but VERIFY on a copy first).
acceptance_criteria: Column typed vector(1024); adapters assert dimension; search-performance.md updated to drop the expression-index plan.

comments.author: darren.mcdowell
comments.text: Discovery during e3v: createIndex (services/indexes.ts) ALREADY creates a per-index partial HNSW expression index (idx_segments_embedding_<id> ON ((embedding::vector(dims))) WHERE index_id=<id>) at index creation. But vectorCandidates orders by s.embedding <=> $1 WITHOUT the cast expression, so the planner cannot match it — the indexes exist but are dead weight (build cost + disk, zero query benefit), and search-performance.md's 'there is no ANN index' claim is stale vs code. Also unaddressed: ef_search >= candidate limit. When typing the column, either wire the query to the index properly (with SET LOCAL hnsw.ef_search) or drop the index creation. Check whether phila-gov-en on dev predates this code (its measured 15s scans suggest no usable index).

title: Index state export endpoint (GET /public/index/:name/documents)
description:
Expose current index state for consumer-side sync (deletion reconciliation + staleness detection).

ENDPOINT: GET /public/index/:name/documents?limit=<N>&after=<external_id>, gated by x-index-key.
RETURNS: { documents: [{ external_id, updated_at, metadata }], next_cursor }
Keyset pagination on immutable external_id, ordered ASC, served by the existing UNIQUE(index_id, external_id) btree. default limit 1000 / max 5000. next_cursor = last external_id when page is full, else null.

KEY DECISION: pagination is mandatory, NOT streaming. Service runs on Lambda behind API Gateway REST v1, which buffers responses and hard-caps at 10MB; an unpaginated dump fails at ~5-26k docs vs the 100k target ceiling. NDJSON/streaming buys nothing end-to-end on this platform.

NO schema change. Two change signals: updated_at (service write time) + full metadata (consumer's own fingerprint channel, e.g. S3 ETag). Internal segment hash stays internal. Sync stays the consumer's job; service exposes state only.

Design: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-25-index-state-export-design.md
Plan:   docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-25-index-state-export-implementation.md
Branch: feat/index-state-export

title: Oversized segments exceed Titan Embed v2 8192-token limit -> ingest 500s (Bedrock ValidationException)
description: During the govsync re-sync (2026-07-07), ~hundreds of phila-gov page upserts failed with HTTP 500. Root cause (from CloudWatch /aws/lambda/dev-pgsearch-lambda-api): 'ValidationException: 400 Bad Request: Too many input tokens. Max input tokens: 8192, request input token count: 8392' thrown in adapter Object.embed -> ingestDocument. Some segments, once the title is prepended (embed text is `${title}\n\n${segment}`) and re-tokenized by Bedrock's tokenizer (which differs from local countTokens), exceed Titan Embed v2's 8192 input-token cap and Bedrock rejects them; the ingest transaction never begins (fails at embed, before any stats work). This is PRE-EXISTING and unrelated to the incremental-BM25-stats work (Plan A) — verified: no Postgres lock/deadlock/timeout errors in the same window. Likely the true cause of the original ~476-page 'residual' (mis-attributed to the refresh stall in the handoff). These specific oversized pages will fail every sync run until fixed. Fix options: (a) reserve headroom in chunkText for the prepended title + a safety margin below 8192; (b) size segments against the Bedrock tokenizer, not the local countTokens; (c) hard-cap/truncate embed input to 8192 tokens as a backstop. Found while running the govsync residual sweep at the end of the pg_cron rollout.

title: Default index config uses local embeddings - unusable against deployed APIs
description:
apps/api/config.ts:7 sets DEFAULT_EMBEDDING.provider = 'local'. The local adapter is a dev-machine-only shim (Transformers.js). Deployed Lambdas bundle only the bedrock adapter, so any index created via POST /private/key/admin/indexes without an explicit embedding config returns 500 on every document ingest.

Example failure: creating 'knowledge-311' with just {name, description} and then pushing a doc returned HTTP 500 from all 900+ attempts in the 311 KB spike. CloudWatch showed: 'embedding provider local is not supported in this deployment. Only bedrock is available. Update the index config and re-ingest.'

The spike worked around this by pinning config.embedding to the same Titan v2 shape used by phila-services-programs. That means every ingestion script has to copy/paste the same bedrock config — easy to forget, impossible to debug from the error code.

Fix options (pick one):
1. Have DEFAULT_CONFIG pull embedding from environment — if ENV suggests 'dev/test/prod' (or a specific env var), default to bedrock
2. Make createIndex reject (with a clear error) when the requested provider is not available in the runtime's registered adapters
3. Ship a second default config 'DEFAULT_DEPLOYED_CONFIG' and wire the admin route to select based on runtime detection
4. Document the gotcha loudly in getting-started.md (worst option, but better than nothing)

File the fix off this issue. Related file: apps/api/services/indexes.ts (createIndex), apps/api/services/ingest.ts (where the 500 is thrown), apps/api/config.ts (DEFAULT_CONFIG).

Introduced: pre-existing, surfaced by the 311 KB ingest spike on 2026-04-14. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-13-311-kb-ingestion.md.

title: Watch for missing table content in 311 KB search eval
description:
Task 3 of the 311 KB ingestion spike stripped tables via remove('table') before toMarkdown() because Turndown's GFM fallback emits raw HTML for tables whose cells contain block-level content (lists, divs). That fallback leaks <table>, <tr>, <td>, <span style=...> into the indexed body, which breaks search.

Salesforce Knowledge articles in this corpus use tables mostly as formatting scaffolds rather than real tabular data, so stripping them seems acceptable. But we should verify during search-quality evaluation: run queries whose answers might live inside a table (eligibility thresholds, fee amounts, service hours by day) and see if recall suffers.

If eval shows we're missing useful table content, revisit the pipeline. Options:
- Custom Turndown rule that always emits GFM tables regardless of cell content
- unwrap non-phrasing cell content (ul/div) before toMarkdown so GFM sees phrasing-only cells
- Post-processing step that extracts cell text into paragraph form

Files:
- apps/api/scripts/ingest-311-kb.ts (parseKbHtml definition)
- docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-13-311-kb-ingestion.md (Known limitations)
- packages/parse/src/markdown.ts (where Turndown+GFM lives)

title: ingestDocument computes the segment diff outside the transaction; concurrent same-external_id writes can store a stale body
description: In apps/api/services/ingest.ts, the changed/unchanged/removedHashes diff (and existing-doc lookup) are read via pool.query BEFORE the transaction begins, and are NOT re-read inside the txn or on a withDeadlockRetry retry. If a concurrent transaction commits a change to the same external_id between that read and the (re)run of the txn body, the stored segment set can reflect a stale diff — the persisted body could diverge from request.body. Stats stay self-consistent (old/new length+term reads are both in-txn), so DF/averages don't corrupt; only the stored segments can be wrong. Pre-existing (predates the incremental-stats feature) but made slightly more prominent by the new retry loop. Narrow: only concurrent writes to the SAME document. Fix options: move the diff read inside the txn (SELECT ... FOR UPDATE on the document row), or add an advisory/row lock on (index_id, external_id) at the start of the txn. Found in the final whole-branch review of feat/incremental-bm25-stats.

title: document_frequency is INTEGER while running-sum columns are BIGINT — revisit type at migration squash
description: term_document_frequencies.document_frequency is INTEGER NOT NULL (carried over from the original matview), capping DF at ~2.1B. The new total_title/body_length + total_segments columns were deliberately BIGINT for headroom. Overflow is not realistic for any real corpus, but the type chain is inconsistent. When the migrations are squashed to a clean baseline (the separate planned follow-up, after dev reaches >=v3), consider promoting document_frequency to BIGINT for consistency. Found in Task 1 code review of feat/incremental-bm25-stats.

comments.author: darren.mcdowell
comments.text: Moot: term_document_frequencies and the running-sum columns were dropped entirely in migration v5 (pgsearch-e3v).

title: govsync bulk sync trips WAF rate limit (HTTP 403 Forbidden) under concurrency
description: During the govsync re-sync (2026-07-07, ~4940 upserts at concurrency ~6), a subset of upserts failed with HTTP 403 {"message":"Forbidden"} — the API Gateway WAF RateLimitRule (raised to 10000/5min in cdk/app.ts) still trips under the sustained bulk burst from a single source IP. These are WAF-level blocks (never reach the Lambda, no Lambda log) and are transient/self-healing (a later sync run re-attempts them). Mitigations to consider: add bounded retry-with-backoff on 403/429 in the govsync upsert client; lower govsync concurrency during bulk loads; or raise the WAF RateLimitRule further for dev. Unrelated to the incremental-BM25-stats work. Found during the pg_cron rollout govsync sweep.

title: Investigate API Gateway 403 throttle rate on dev pgsearch
description:
phila-gov-en full crawl of phila.gov hit ~22 403 'Forbidden' responses from API Gateway / WAF even at concurrency 2 (~2-3 req/sec sustained). The first crawl at concurrency 4 hit one within ~100 docs. Worth checking:

1. AWS WAF rate-based rules on the dev API stage (default: 2000 req/5min from same IP)
2. API Gateway throttle settings on the /public/index/:name/documents route
3. Per-key throttle (if any) on the index_key auth flow

Goal: understand the actual ceiling so future crawler concurrency tuning is informed, and so the retry policy in pgsearch-<TBD> uses a sane delay.

Spec reference: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-06-phila-gov-en-ingestion-design.md

title: Fix TS1343 on import.meta.url in 311 KB ingest script
description:
apps/api/scripts/ingest-311-kb.ts:158 uses 'import.meta.url === pathToFileURL(process.argv[1]).href' as a module-entry guard. Under strict 'tsc --noEmit' across the api package, this triggers TS1343 (The 'import.meta' meta-property is only allowed when the '--module' option is 'es2020', 'es2022', 'esnext', 'system', 'node16', 'nodenext', or 'preserve'). Vite-node compiles and runs it fine, which is why vitest stays green and the script executes correctly — but CI or any strict type-check pass will fail.

Fix is a module target bump in apps/api/tsconfig.json (or root tsconfig.json) to module: ES2020 or later. Keep an eye out for regressions in other files that might rely on CommonJS-style module behavior under the old setting.

Introduced in Task 1 of the 311 KB ingest spike (branch wip/311-kb-ingestion). Agreed to defer to post-spike cleanup.

Files:
- apps/api/scripts/ingest-311-kb.ts (line 158, the guard)
- apps/api/tsconfig.json (target of the fix)
- tsconfig.json (alternative target)

title: Migrate lexical pass to SQL ts_rank_cd; delete BM25F stats subsystem
description:
Gated on pgsearch-qpp go decision. Collapses the lexical pass to one indexed SQL query and deletes the most complex subsystem in the service, aligning config surface with the product goal (opinionated, near-zero tuning).

Implementation notes:
- Ingest: build tsvectors with setweight (title=A, body=D). Backfill migration re-writes existing tsvectors from stored title/body — no re-embedding needed. Mind the 30s Lambda migration timeout (db/migrate.ts); backfill may belong in a pg_cron one-shot like reconcile does.
- Search (services/search.ts): replace bm25 candidate query with GIN match + ORDER BY ts_rank_cd(weight_array, tsvector, tsquery, normalization_flag) DESC LIMIT 200. Delete parseTsvectorTf, stemQueryTerms, computeBM25F path, df lookup.
- Delete: term_document_frequencies table + incremental maintenance (services/stats.ts), reconcile_index_stats machinery (services/reconcile.ts + pg_cron job), bm25_k1/bm25_b/field_weights config knobs (field weighting moves to the ts_rank_cd weight array), avg_title_length/avg_body_length/total_documents stats columns if nothing else consumes them.
- Config surface after: rrf_k, rrf_weights, score floors, text_search_config.
- Related issues likely mooted by this deletion: pgsearch-bto (document_frequency INTEGER vs BIGINT), the last_refreshed_at watermark bug, pgsearch-40e context. Check and close/link at execution time.
- Docs: search.md scoring-parameters table and 'BM25F statistics are maintained incrementally' section, search-performance.md known-issues (LIMIT 200 bug becomes unexpressible), HANDOFF-2026-06-30-incremental-bm25-stats.md is historical.
- Keep response shape identical; scores remain RRF rank-derived.
acceptance_criteria: Lexical pass is one SQL query; stats subsystem removed; eval numbers on the migrated pass match the pgsearch-qpp go decision; docs updated.

comments.author: darren.mcdowell
comments.text: Scope decision: keeping setweight on-the-fly in the search query (as validated in the A/B — 0.554s avg, faster than the JS path) instead of ingest-time weighted tsvectors + backfill. Revisit only if lexical latency degrades at larger corpora.

comments.author: darren.mcdowell
comments.text: Shipped on eval-harness and deployed to dev. Lexical pass is one SQL query (ts_rank_cd, setweight on-the-fly, ORDER BY before LIMIT 200). Deleted: term_document_frequencies + incremental maintenance (services/stats.ts), reconcile (service, route, client method, pg_cron job — verified unscheduled on dev), bm25_k1/bm25_b knobs, stats columns (migration v5). Config surface now: field_weights, rrf_k, rrf_weights, floors, text_search_config. Suite 193/193 green incl. Bedrock e2e. Post-migration capture final-tsrank-default is 1.00/1.00 identical to the validated variant-tsrank across all categories and modes. Also removed obsolete watermark columns, so the last_refreshed_at known issue is moot.

title: Corpus near-duplicate pages skew lexical ranking
description: The phila-gov-en corpus contains near-duplicate documents — same or near-same title and body at multiple URLs (e.g. 'Building permit materials | Department of Licenses and Inspections' appears as at least two documents, 'Apply for water bill customer assistance' as several, 'Pay a water bill' twice). Observed during the pgsearch-qpp lexical A/B: duplicates occupy multiple top-10 slots for 'L&I building permit' and 'pay water bill', amplifying scorer quirks and displacing distinct relevant pages. Per-document dedup (maxChunksPerDoc) can't help because these are distinct documents with distinct external_ids (URLs). Investigate whether they are crawler artifacts (same page reachable via multiple routes/query strings — check URL canonicalization in apps/crawler) or genuinely duplicated pages on phila.gov. Fix belongs at ingest/crawl (canonical URL or content-hash dedup), not in scoring. Evidence: apps/api/scripts/eval/captures/variant-tsrank.json top-10s for the queries above.
acceptance_criteria: Duplicate documents identified and either canonicalized at crawl or deduped at ingest; eval top-10s for 'L&I building permit' and 'pay water bill' show no repeated near-identical pages.

title: Sub-partitioning columns for index isolation flexibility
description:
Long-term flexibility around index isolation: an indexed column on documents (or a partition_key + sort_key pair) would let consumers filter/range-query within a single index. Currently content_type lives in metadata jsonb (no index). YAGNI for the phila-gov-en build (we use metadata only) but Darren flagged this as the long-term design direction.

Open questions:
- Single content_type text column (low-cardinality filter) vs DynamoDB-style partition+sort
- Migration path for existing indexes
- API surface for filter/range queries in /public/search/:name

Not urgent; revisit when consumer access patterns are concrete.

Spec reference: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-06-phila-gov-en-ingestion-design.md

title: Migration runner is non-transactional; v3 (and similar multi-step migrations) can't be cleanly re-run after partial failure
description: apps/api/db/migrate.ts applies migration.sql and the schema_migrations INSERT as two separate queries (not one txn). If the process dies mid-migration, the version isn't recorded and the next cold start re-runs the whole SQL block. For simple CREATE IF NOT EXISTS migrations (v1/v2) that's fine, but migration v3 does an inherently sequential matview->table conversion (CREATE _tbl, INSERT-SELECT from matview, DROP matview, RENAME _tbl). If it aborts after the matview is dropped/renamed, a re-run's INSERT-SELECT reads from the now-base-table and the final RENAME collides -> manual cleanup required. Partial mitigation already shipped in 53f3469 (IF EXISTS/IF NOT EXISTS + leading DROP TABLE _tbl covers the failed-at-step-1 case). Real fix options: (a) wrap each migration's sql + schema_migrations INSERT in a single transaction in migrate.ts; or (b) make migrations individually idempotent. Revisit before the planned squash-to-baseline migration. Found in Task 1 code review of feat/incremental-bm25-stats.

comments.author: darren.mcdowell
comments.text: Exposure reduced by the baseline refactor: migrations.ts is now a single declarative baseline (v5) + future imperative change-sets, so the multi-step v3 re-run hazard no longer exists in the shipped set. The runner itself is still non-transactional — still worth wrapping each migration in BEGIN/COMMIT before the next multi-statement change-set (v6+) lands.

title: Inject index config into search/rag, drop hybridSearch re-fetch
description:
hybridSearch (services/search.ts:106-109) re-queries search_indexes for 'config, total_documents, avg_title_length, avg_body_length' — all already on the resolved index that the search route holds (routes/search.ts, c.get('index')). Same redundant-fetch pattern just fixed in ingest (commit 4e16991).

Fix: thread these from the route into hybridSearch instead of re-querying, mirroring how the adapter is already injected.

Ripple (larger than ingest): hybridSearch is also called by runRag (services/rag.ts:27), which only receives indexId. So the rag route must pass index config/stats into runRag, which passes them through to hybridSearch. Plus ~30 hybridSearch call sites across test/search.test.ts, test/rag.test.ts, test/e2e-hybrid-search.test.ts.

Note: index stats (total_documents, avg_*) are loaded by auth at request start; within a single request they don't change, so passing the context index's stats is equivalent to the re-fetch (no staleness concern). TDD: existing search/rag suites are the regression net; add a test proving config/stats come from the param (e.g. pass a distinctive text_search_config and assert tsquery/scoring reflects it).

title: A/B lexical pass: BM25F-in-JS vs weighted ts_rank_cd-in-SQL under RRF
description:
Decision experiment for the lexical-pass simplification. Direction agreed with Darren 2026-07-09: the service's goal is OpenSearch-grade hybrid search with opinionated near-zero config; BM25F was built to mimic why people pick OpenSearch, but in a hybrid+RRF system the lexical pass is a co-ranker whose real job is MEMBERSHIP (exact tokens embeddings fumble: form numbers, street names, jargon), not scoring precision — RRF flattens magnitudes into ranks and the docs' own analysis (search-performance.md) shows membership errors cost ~40x ordering errors. We replicated BM25's formula but not its index; the index (rank-before-truncate) was the point.

Hypothesis: one SQL query — GIN match, ORDER BY ts_rank_cd with length-normalization flag, LIMIT 200, using setweight'd tsvectors (title=A, body=D, weight array preserving the title-3x opinion) — is within eval noise of the current JS BM25F under RRF fusion. plainto_tsquery AND-semantics blunts the IDF loss (every candidate already contains the rare term); vector pass co-carries semantics.

Method: implement the SQL variant behind a flag or branch, run the pgsearch-6rv eval set both ways, compare overlap/rank-correlation on final HYBRID rankings (that's the product surface). Note: the SQL variant also inherently fixes the LIMIT-200-no-ORDER-BY membership bug, so some divergence is the bug being fixed, not quality loss — eyeball the diffs, don't just trust correlation numbers.

If the hypothesis fails: fallback is keeping BM25F but adding ORDER BY ts_rank_cd before the LIMIT as candidate pre-ranking (membership fix only). Escape hatch for ordering-quality gaps at any point: cross-encoder rerank of fused top-50 (Cohere Rerank on Bedrock) — do NOT re-litigate lexical scoring math.
acceptance_criteria: Eval comparison numbers + a written go/no-go on pgsearch-<migrate> with Darren's sign-off.

comments.author: darren.mcdowell
comments.text: A/B results (captures committed on eval-harness): noise floor baseline-vs-control = 1.00 everywhere (deterministic), so all variant divergence is signal. Hybrid overlap@10 0.90 overall; semantic mode 1.00 (untouched, control valid). Divergence concentrated in head (0.72) and exact-token (0.88) — the deep-pool queries. Eyeball verdicts: 'building permit' decisively BETTER under tsrank (control top-10 was missing the canonical 'Get a Building Permit' page entirely — membership bug in action; variant has it at #2). 'pay water bill' comparable. 'L&I building permit' mildly worse ordering under tsrank (title-stuffed near-duplicate 'Building permit materials' pages rank 1-2; canonical at #4 vs control #1) — cover-density lacking IDF, amplified by corpus near-dups. Latency: tsrank 0.554s avg vs bm25f 0.868s in bm25 mode (skips tsvector shipping + JS scoring + df roundtrip) despite ranking all matches. Recommendation: GO, pending Darren sign-off. Note for e3v: corpus near-duplicate pages (same title, multiple URLs) amplify scorer quirks — consider a dedup issue.

comments.author: darren.mcdowell
comments.text: Darren: GO. Proceeding with pgsearch-e3v migration.

title: Programs and HR job-spec pages produce null titles, fail ingest with 400
description:
After phila-gov-en full crawl, 6 docs failed ingest with 400 'Missing required field: title' from the API. The parse pipelines produced null titles for these URLs. Pattern groups:

1. Some /programs/ leaves where parseProgram's selector .program header h1 fails:
   - https://www.phila.gov/programs/philly-keep-on-loving/
   - https://www.phila.gov/programs/vision-zero-philadelphia/
   - https://www.phila.gov/programs/treephilly/
   - https://www.phila.gov/programs/mortgage-and-tax-foreclosure-prevention-program/

2. Department HR job-specs (legacy .htm files):
   - https://www.phila.gov/departments/office-of-human-resources/job-specs/4c02.htm
   - https://www.phila.gov/departments/office-of-human-resources/job-specs/3h67.htm

Fix options:
A. Pipelines: add og:title fallback to parseProgram, parseDocument, parseService (currently only parseDepartment and parseNews fall back)
B. Crawler integration: drop docs with null title before sink call, count as parse:no-title (separate from no-content)
C. Both — fall back where possible, drop where not

Recommended: B as a safety rail (we should never POST a null title), plus A for parseProgram specifically (the 4 program failures are all real content with og:title set).

Spec reference: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-06-phila-gov-en-ingestion-design.md
Plan reference: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-06-phila-gov-en-ingestion.md

title: Test infra: app-level memoized pool leaks + env-ordering coupling in app.request integration tests
description:
Surfaced during code review of the index state export route tests (feat/index-state-export).

The app.request integration tests (test/documents.test.ts 'GET ... route' block, and the pre-existing test/rag.test.ts 'ragAuth integration' block) drive the real Hono app, which resolves its pool via db/pool.ts getPool() — a module-level memoized pool. Two issues, both PRE-EXISTING (inherited from rag.test.ts), neither a regression:

1. Leaked handle: setup.ts closePool() only ends the TEST pool (getTestPool). The app-level pool opened by getPool() during these tests is never closed, lingering as an open handle after the suite.

2. Hidden ordering coupling: the tests set process.env.DB_* in beforeAll, but those writes only take effect if getPool()'s memoized pool hasn't already been constructed by an earlier-running test file. Currently safe only because rag.test.ts sets identical values and vitest fileParallelism:false keeps execution sequential/single-process. The coupling is invisible and fragile.

Possible fix: expose a closeAppPool()/resetPool() in db/pool.ts and call it in the integration blocks' afterAll; and/or centralize the DB_* test env wiring (e.g. in test/setup.ts or a shared helper) so individual test files don't each poke process.env. Apply to both documents.test.ts and rag.test.ts.

Low priority — tests pass and are correct today; this is robustness/cleanup.

title: Verify documents discovery via crawler link-walk; supplemental script if needed
description:
During spec probing for phila-gov-en, the /documents/ index page surfaced only ONE leaf URL in plain HTML (city-of-philadelphia-organization-chart). Either the listing is paginated, JS-rendered, or genuinely sparse.

After the first full crawl of phila-gov-en, check byType.documents.ingested. If the count is unreasonably small relative to known document volume, build a supplemental discovery script (e.g., paged listing fetch, or out-of-band document API) similar to the 311 KB pattern.

Don't build pre-emptively. Verify post-crawl.

Spec reference: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-06-phila-gov-en-ingestion-design.md

title: News search: time-relevance scoring boost
description:
Recent news articles should rank higher than older ones. metadata.published_date is already stamped on news docs by the pgsearch crawler (date parsed from /YYYY-MM-DD-slug/ URL). Need to surface this as a ranking signal in hybrid search.

Approach options:
- Decay factor on RRF score using (today - published_date)
- Boost in BM25F for recent docs only
- Per-index 'time decay' config (alpha, half-life) for indexes that opt in

Out of scope for the phila-gov-en build. Track here so we don't lose it.

Spec reference: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-06-phila-gov-en-ingestion-design.md
